6.anchor: what is going to happen to the ungodly;
6.note: Some manuscripts an example to those who were to be ungodly

9.anchor: the Lord knows how to rescue the godly from trials,

10.note: Greek who go after the flesh

13.anchor: to revel in the daytime. They are blots and blemishes, reveling in their deceptions,

14.anchor: They have eyes full of adultery,
14.note: Or eyes full of an adulteress

19.note: For the contextual rendering of the Greek word doulos

1.note: For the contextual rendering of the Greek word doulos

10.note: Or brothers and sisters. In New Testament usage, depending on the context, the plural Greek word

10.anchor: will be burned up and dissolved, and the earth and the works that are done on it will be exposed.
10.note: Greek found will be burned up
